commonProblems.h2: Jaguar XJ Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: Jaguar XJ reliability is largely dictated by the AJ-V8 petrol series, the AJD-V6 diesel family, and the later Ingenium four-cylinder units. Owners typically face timing chain degradation, EGR cooler cracking, and supercharger wear as mileage accumulates. Every rebuilt Jaguar XJ engine supplied through our network includes upgraded internal components designed to permanently address these original failure points, backed by a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the breakdowns below to compare repair costs against full replacement and make an informed financial decision.

problems.h4: Jaguar XJ Timing Chain Failure - Why AJ-V8 & AJ126 Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Jaguar XJ Portfolio, Sovereign, XJR, Sport (2009-2019, AJ133 5.0L & AJ126 3.0L SC)
problems.rootCause: Plastic timing chain guides become brittle under thermal cycling, while hydraulic tensioners lose pressure, allowing chain stretch and potential catastrophic valve/piston contact.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Timing Chain Kit Upgrade
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replacing chains, guides, tensioners, and VVT sprockets with upgraded metal/reinforced components.
repairOptions.longevity: 100,000+ miles if completed before stretch; strictly preventative.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Removing head, repairing damaged valves/guides, machining block deck, and reinstalling with new timing kit.
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if bottom-end damage is minimal; high labour risk.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of bench-tested rebuilt engine with reinforced chains, tensioners, and 12‑month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: Factory-spec or improved; permanent solution for catastrophic failure.

problems.recommendation: On a Jaguar XJ exceeding 70,000 miles, preventative chain replacement is highly recommended over waiting for audible symptoms. If the AJ133 or AJ126 has already suffered valve damage or seized, replacement is the only financially sensible route given the labour intensity of in-situ repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt AJ133 engine prices for your Jaguar XJ

problems.h4: Jaguar XJ EGR Cooler Cracking - Why AJD-V6 Diesel Engines Fail Under Load
problems.rootCause: Thermal expansion and exhaust soot buildup cause the internal EGR cooler matrix to fracture, allowing coolant to leak into the intake manifold and combustion chambers, leading to hydrolock.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Removing and replacing the cracked EGR cooler and valve with revised JLR-spec components, plus cooling system flush.
repairOptions.longevity: Resolves leak if caught early before cylinder ingestion; standard maintenance fix.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Cylinder Liner & Head Gasket Repair
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Block disassembly, liner inspection/replacement, head skimming, and gasket installation.
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only for partial coolant ingestion; labour-heavy with mixed long-term reliability.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Diesel Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of remanufactured AJD-V6 with reinforced internals, updated emissions compatibility, and 12‑month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: Eliminates all internal coolant damage; best value post-failure for the 3.0L diesel.

problems.recommendation: We strongly advise replacing the EGR cooler at the first sign of unexplained coolant loss or white exhaust smoke. Once hydrolock occurs or cylinder walls score, repair costs on the Jaguar XJ 3.0L diesel consistently exceed the value of a rebuilt replacement unit.
problems.cta: Get quotes for AJD-V6 306DT replacement for your Jaguar XJ

problems.h4: Jaguar XJ Supercharger Failure - Why AJ133SC V8 Engines Lose Boost Pressure
problems.affectedModels: Jaguar XJ XJR, Supersport, Autobiography (2009-2019, AJ133SC 5.0L SC)
problems.rootCause: The rubber supercharger drive coupler degrades and the magnetic clutch bearing wears under high thermal stress, causing slippage, metallic rattling under load, and eventual loss of forced induction pressure.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Supercharger Coupler & Clutch Bearing Kit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Dropping the blower from the plenum, replacing coupler sleeves, bearings, and seals without full unit removal.
repairOptions.longevity: 40,000-60,000 miles; standard proactive maintenance for high-performance Jaguar XJ V8s.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Removing, disassembling, replacing internal lobes/rotors, resealing, and bench-testing pressure output.
repairOptions.longevity: Restores factory boost; expensive but avoids full engine swap if snout is intact.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Supercharged V8
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of fully rebuilt AJ133SC engine with new supercharger assembly, upgraded cooling, and 12‑month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: Complete powertrain renewal; recommended after internal snout bearing failure.

problems.recommendation: On a Jaguar XJ XJR, the supercharger coupler should be replaced proactively during major service intervals. If you hear metallic rattling on acceleration or experience limp mode, a specialist clutch rebuild is highly cost-effective. Complete engine replacement is only necessary if snout bearing failure has caused debris ingestion into the intake valves.
problems.cta: Compare AJ133SC supercharged engine prices for your Jaguar XJ

problems.group: Jaguar XJ Ingenium Timing Chain Failure
problems.h4: Jaguar XJ Ingenium Timing Chain Failure - Why 2.0L Four-Cylinder Engines Rattle Cold
problems.affectedModels: Jaguar XJ Executive, R-Sport, Portfolio (2015-2019, AJ200D 2.0L Diesel & AJ200P 2.0L Petrol)
problems.rootCause: Early-generation Ingenium oil pump drive gears and chain tensioners suffer from inadequate lubrication flow and soft metallurgy, leading to rapid chain stretch and pronounced cold-start rattle.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Chain & Tensioner Upgrade Kit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Installing revised metal tensioners, heavy-duty chains, and upgraded oil pump drive gears to JLR specification.
repairOptions.longevity: Resolves rattle if caught early; permanent fix for early-production blocks.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Ingenium Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of fully remanufactured AJ200 series with updated internals, dyno testing, and 12‑month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: Factory standard or improved; only viable option once chain jumps or valves bend.

problems.recommendation: The Jaguar XJ 2.0 Ingenium units require strict adherence to manufacturer service intervals. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, a preventative chain kit installation is significantly cheaper than a full engine replacement. If the engine has already suffered internal impact damage, replacement is unavoidable and offers the strongest warranty protection.
problems.cta: Compare AJ200D/AJ200P engine quotes for your Jaguar XJ

problems.h4: Jaguar XJ Oil Consumption Failure - Why AJ41 & Early AJ133 V8s Burn Oil
problems.affectedModels: Jaguar XJ Sovereign, Premium Luxury, Sport (2003-2012, AJ41 4.2L & early AJ133 5.0L NA)
problems.rootCause: Piston ring lands soften and oil control rings carbonize due to combustion temperatures and extended service intervals, allowing oil to bypass into the combustion chambers and trigger catalytic converter damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fitting an external breather catch-can system and switching to high-zinc specification oil.
repairOptions.longevity: Mitigates minor consumption and reduces carbon buildup; not a mechanical cure.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Piston Ring & Bore Honing Overhaul
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine strip-down, honing cylinder bores, fitting revised steel piston rings and upgraded bearings.
repairOptions.longevity: Extends life by 50,000+ miles; viable only if cylinder walls remain undamaged.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned V8 Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of fully machined and rebuilt AJ-V8 with reinforced pistons, rings, and full warranty coverage.
repairOptions.longevity: Comprehensive solution; recommended once blue smoke or compression loss is evident.

problems.recommendation: If your Jaguar XJ V8 burns more than one litre per 1,000 miles, an intermediate ring overhaul is possible only on engines caught early. Once bore scoring occurs, replacement is the only reliable option to restore factory compression and protect downstream exhaust components from oil contamination.
problems.cta: Get rebuilt V8 engine quotes for your Jaguar XJ

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Jaguar XJ
finalCta.paragraph: The Jaguar XJ is a precision-engineered executive saloon, but known vulnerabilities in timing systems, emissions coolers, and supercharger drives can quickly escalate into sudden, expensive breakdowns. Rather than accepting main dealer premiums or risking unguaranteed used units, EngineMarket connects you directly with vetted UK Jaguar XJ engine specialists. Every rebuilt replacement engine we list features upgraded components targeting these exact failure points, is dyno-tested before dispatch, and arrives with nationwide delivery and a minimum 12‑month warranty. Compare transparent quotes today, protect your vehicle’s residual value, and return to the road without the dealership markup.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Jaguar XJ Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket’s UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Jaguar XJ and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Jaguar XJ Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every engine displacement officially fitted to the UK Jaguar XJ is listed below, complete with matching engine codes, production years and known failure points to help you verify compatibility before sourcing a replacement.

items.title: Jaguar XJ 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The compact four-cylinder diesel arrived in the facelifted X351 generation as Jaguar’s response to tightening ULEZ regulations and corporate fleet emissions targets. Built around the modern Ingenium architecture, this unit delivers responsive torque and refined highway cruising while maintaining a lightweight footprint under the bonnet.
commonFailurePoints: Early production timing chain tensioner wear
high-pressure fuel pump cam follower degradation
items.cta: Get quotes for Jaguar XJ 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Jaguar XJ 2.7L Diesel Engines
items.description: Introduced during the early X350 launch, this co-developed V6 established the modern diesel efficiency benchmark for the luxury saloon before being phased out for larger capacity units. Its compact aluminium block and variable geometry turbo setup delivered smooth power delivery suited to grand touring, though early emissions hardware proved prone to soot accumulation.
commonFailurePoints: oil filter housing cracks leading to sudden pressure loss
items.cta: Get quotes for Jaguar XJ 2.7L diesel engines

items.title: Jaguar XJ 3.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: Forming the backbone of the diesel range across both X350 facelifts and the entire X351 lifecycle, this V6 evolved from a single-turbo TDV6 to a twin-turbo sequential SDV6 for flagship executive variants. The architecture balances robust torque availability with advanced emissions controls, though the complex cooling systems require diligent maintenance to avoid catastrophic coolant ingress.
commonFailurePoints: EGR cooler internal matrix cracking causing coolant ingestion
items.cta: Get quotes for Jaguar XJ 3.0L diesel engines

items.title: Jaguar XJ 4.4L Diesel Engines
items.description: A rare, short-lived option offered primarily in the early X350 generation to bridge the gap between standard diesel efficiency and flagship torque delivery. Featuring twin sequential turbochargers and advanced high-pressure common rail injection, it provided effortless overtaking power but suffered from complex ancillary routing that increased long-term servicing costs.
commonFailurePoints: Twin turbocharger bearing failure under sustained high-load conditions
items.cta: Get quotes for Jaguar XJ 4.4L diesel engines

items.title: Jaguar XJ 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: Debuting in the final X351 facelift cycle, this turbocharged four-cylinder replaced the traditional entry-level V6 to meet urban emissions standards without sacrificing cabin refinement. Featuring direct injection and lightweight construction, it prioritises low-speed torque and clean air zone compliance, making it a practical choice for executive commuters.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain guide fracture due to oil starvation
items.cta: Compare Jaguar XJ 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Jaguar XJ 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: Evolving from a naturally aspirated V6 into a supercharged alternative, this compact six-cylinder delivers spirited acceleration while offering better front-axle weight distribution than the larger V8 options. The later supercharged variant introduced a roots-style compressor for instant throttle response, though the added forced induction components introduced new wear points around drive couplers.
commonFailurePoints: timing chain tensioner hydraulic leak causing cold-start rattle
items.cta: Compare Jaguar XJ 3.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Jaguar XJ 4.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: Serving as the definitive naturally aspirated and supercharged V8 for the X350 generation, this unit combined traditional torque-rich character with modern variable valve timing and electronic throttle control. The reinforced aluminium block and composite intake manifolds provided smooth, linear power delivery, though early tensioner feed restrictions required later factory corrections.
commonFailurePoints: supercharger bearing wear under sustained high-RPM load
items.cta: Compare Jaguar XJ 4.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Jaguar XJ 5.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: Flagship of the X351 range, this all-aluminium V8 architecture replaced the older 4.2-litre unit to deliver enhanced rigidity, direct injection, and significantly improved thermal management. Available in naturally aspirated and supercharged configurations, it offers effortless motorway cruising, though the complex timing chain routing behind the cylinder banks demands strict oil change intervals.
commonFailurePoints: Plastic timing chain guide fragmentation causing catastrophic stretch
piston ring land cracking leading to excessive oil consumption
items.cta: Compare Jaguar XJ 5.0L petrol engine prices

groups.title: Jaguar XJ Hybrid / MHEV Engine Sizes

items.title: Jaguar XJ 3.0L MHEV Engines
items.description: Introduced exclusively for the final production runs of the X351, this inline-six mild-hybrid architecture integrates a 48-volt belt-starter generator to provide torque fill during acceleration and seamless stop-start functionality. Designed to optimise low-speed emissions without sacrificing traditional torque delivery, it bridges combustion performance with modern electrification efficiency. Not sure which engine size your Jaguar XJ needs? Enter your registration number above - we’ll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Jaguar XJ specialists.

engineYears.h2: Jaguar XJ Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This technical timeline covers every major engine change across the Jaguar XJ's UK production run from 2003 to 2019, organised so owners can quickly identify their exact engine code and compare rebuilt replacement prices from trusted UK specialists.

years.description: The aluminium-intensive X350 chassis launched with refined naturally aspirated and supercharged AJ-V8 petrol engines, establishing the modern XJ's luxury performance credentials.
keyChanges: All-new aluminium monocoque X350 platform replacing steel X308 architecture
Introduction of AJ41 4.2L V8 naturally aspirated and AJ41SC supercharged engines
Drive-by-wire throttle and composite intake manifolds for refined power delivery
Premium Luxury, Sovereign and XJR trim levels established at launch
mainEngines: AJ41 - 4.2L petrol (Executive, Sovereign, Premium Luxury)
AJ41SC - 4.2L supercharged petrol (XJR flagship performance variant)
AJ34 - 4.2L early production V8 (pre-2004 calibration variants)
knownFor: Silky V8 refinement and effortless motorway cruising character
Early Nikasil cylinder liner concerns on pre-2004 AJ34 blocks
Crankshaft pulley harmonic damper separation around 70k miles
Strong collector interest in well-maintained X350 examples today
years.cta: Get quotes for 2003-2006 Jaguar XJ replacement engines
years.ticker: Jaguar XJ 4.2 V8 engine replacement • XJR supercharger bearing repair • AJ41 timing chain tensioner upgrade

years.description: The 2007 facelift introduced Jaguar's first diesel engine to the XJ range, responding to UK fleet demand for improved fuel economy without sacrificing executive refinement.
keyChanges: Introduction of 2.7L AJD-V6 TDV6 diesel engine for UK/European markets
Minor exterior refresh with updated grille and LED lighting signatures
Revised interior trim options and enhanced infotainment connectivity
4.4L V8 diesel (448DT) offered briefly in select high-spec variants
mainEngines: 276DT - 2.7L diesel (Executive, Sovereign, Premium Luxury TDV6)
AJ41 - 4.2L petrol (continued carryover from launch)
AJ41SC - 4.2L supercharged petrol (XJR performance flagship)
448DT - 4.4L twin-turbo diesel (rare Sovereign/Premium Luxury application)
knownFor: 2.7L TDV6 delivers strong torque and 40+ mpg motorway economy
Cylinder liner sealing washer failure risk on early 276DT units
EGR cooler cracking and oil filter housing leaks common at higher mileage
Final X350 production years offer strong value for budget-conscious buyers
years.cta: Compare Jaguar XJ 2007-2009 diesel engine prices
years.ticker: Jaguar XJ 2.7 TDV6 engine replacement • XJ diesel EGR cooler repair • 276DT cylinder liner seal fix

years.preview: X351 Launch: AJ133 5.0L V8 & AJD-V6 Diesel
years.description: The all-new X351 generation debuted with the landmark AJ133 5.0L V8 and refined AJD-V6 3.0L diesel, setting new benchmarks for performance and efficiency in the executive saloon segment.
keyChanges: Complete platform change to X351 with revised suspension geometry and weight distribution
Introduction of AJ133 5.0L V8 naturally aspirated and AJ133SC supercharged engines
AJD-V6 306DT 3.0L TDV6 diesel replaces outgoing 2.7L unit with improved refinement
New interior architecture with rising gear selector and touchscreen infotainment

AJ133SC - 5.0L supercharged petrol (XJR, Supersport performance variants)
306DT - 3.0L diesel TDV6 (mainstream Portfolio, Sovereign, Executive variants)
30DDTX - 3.0L diesel SDV6 twin-turbo (high-spec LWB and Autobiography)
knownFor: AJ133 delivers effortless V8 refinement with direct injection efficiency gains
Early AJ133 units susceptible to plastic timing chain guide degradation (50k-80k miles)
306DT EGR cooler cracking remains the dominant diesel failure pattern
X351 launch models command strong residual values when well maintained

faq.h2: Jaguar XJ Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Jaguar XJ engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Jaguar XJ engine replacement costs typically range from £1,500 for a used unit to £7,200 for a fully rebuilt AJ133SC supercharged V8. Supply-only rebuilt engines average £2,500-£4,800 depending on engine code, with supply-and-fit adding £800-£1,500 labour. Prices vary by variant, mileage and specialist location.
keyPoints: AJ200D 2.0L diesel rebuilt units start around £1,500 supply only
AJ133 5.0L V8 petrol replacements average £2,600-£4,800 rebuilt
Supply-and-fit labour typically adds £800-£1,500 to engine price
Supercharged AJ133SC variants command premium pricing due to complexity
items.cta: Compare Jaguar XJ engine replacement prices from UK specialists today

items.question: Is the Jaguar XJ AJ133 5.0L V8 engine reliable?
items.answer: The Jaguar XJ AJ133 5.0L V8 is refined and powerful but requires strict oil change intervals to protect timing components. Early units (2009-2012) are prone to plastic timing chain guide degradation around 50k-80k miles. Later builds with revised guides show improved longevity when properly maintained.
keyPoints: AJ133 timing chain guides can fragment causing catastrophic stretch risk
Strict 10,000-mile oil changes essential to prevent premature wear
Rebuilt units with upgraded metal guides offer improved long-term reliability
Supercharged AJ133SC variants need additional supercharger clutch monitoring
items.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt AJ133 Jaguar XJ engines with upgraded timing components

items.question: What causes timing chain failure on Jaguar XJ engines?
items.answer: Jaguar XJ timing chain failure typically stems from plastic guide degradation and hydraulic tensioner wear, particularly on AJ133, AJ126 and early AJD-V6 engines. Cold-start rattling is the key warning sign. Failure usually occurs between 50,000-80,000 miles if preventative maintenance is overlooked.
keyPoints: Plastic timing chain guides become brittle under thermal cycling stress
Hydraulic tensioners lose pressure allowing chain stretch and misalignment
Cold-start metallic rattle indicates imminent guide failure requiring inspection
Upgraded metal guide kits available for preventative replacement during rebuilds
items.cta: Compare Jaguar XJ timing chain upgrade kits and rebuilt engine options

items.question: Are Jaguar XJ diesel engines more reliable than petrol?
items.answer: Jaguar XJ diesel engines (AJD-V6/306DT) offer strong economy but face EGR cooler cracking and turbo actuator issues. Petrol AJ133 units deliver refinement but require vigilant timing chain maintenance. Neither is universally more reliable - maintenance history and engine code matter more than fuel type for Jaguar XJ longevity.
keyPoints: TDV6/SDV6 diesels suffer EGR cooler cracking around 70,000 miles typically
AJ133 petrol engines need strict oil changes to protect timing components
Diesel DPF regeneration failures common on short-journey urban driving patterns
Both fuel types benefit from rebuilt units with upgraded failure-point components
items.cta: Get quotes for Jaguar XJ diesel or petrol replacement engines today

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in a high-mileage Jaguar XJ?
items.answer: Replacing a Jaguar XJ engine is often worthwhile if the chassis, interior and electronics remain sound. A rebuilt engine with 12-month warranty typically costs £2,500-£4,800, far less than vehicle replacement. Focus on X351 models (2009-2019) with strong residual values and parts availability for best return on investment.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engine costs significantly less than purchasing a replacement Jaguar XJ
X351 chassis models retain strong values making engine replacement economically sensible
12-month unlimited mileage warranty on rebuilt units protects your investment
Supply-and-fit packages simplify the process with single-point responsibility coverage
items.cta: Find out if your high-mileage Jaguar XJ is worth engine replacement

items.question: What are the most common Jaguar XJ engine problems to watch for?
items.answer: The most frequent Jaguar XJ engine issues include AJ133/AJ126 timing chain guide failure, AJD-V6 EGR cooler cracking, and supercharger coupler wear on performance variants. Cold-start rattling, unexplained coolant loss, or boost pressure loss are key warning signs requiring immediate specialist diagnosis before catastrophic damage occurs.
keyPoints: AJ133/AJ126 timing chain rattle indicates plastic guide degradation needing attention
TDV6/SDV6 EGR cooler cracks cause coolant ingestion and potential hydrolock
Supercharged variants develop coupler wear causing boost loss and metallic noise
Ingenium 2.0L units suffer early tensioner failures requiring preventative replacement
items.cta: Get diagnostic quotes for your Jaguar XJ engine problem today

items.question: How long should a Jaguar XJ engine last with proper maintenance?
items.answer: With strict adherence to 10,000-mile oil changes and timely timing component service, Jaguar XJ engines can reliably reach 120,000-150,000 miles. AJ41 4.2L V8 units often exceed 150k miles with care, while AJ133 and AJD-V6 engines require proactive timing chain/EGR maintenance to achieve similar longevity without major repairs.
keyPoints: AJ41 4.2L V8 engines often exceed 150,000 miles with diligent servicing
AJ133 timing chain service recommended preventatively at 60,000 miles intervals
Diesel AJD-V6 units need EGR cooler inspection around 70,000 miles typically
Full service history significantly extends Jaguar XJ engine lifespan and resale value
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Jaguar XJ engines designed for extended longevity and reliability

items.question: Can I upgrade my Jaguar XJ engine during replacement?
items.answer: Upgrading your Jaguar XJ engine during replacement is possible but complex. Swapping an AJD-V6 diesel for an AJ133 petrol, or adding supercharger components, requires ECU remapping, wiring harness changes and chassis compatibility verification. Most owners opt for like-for-like rebuilt units with upgraded internal components addressing known failure points instead.
keyPoints: Like-for-like rebuilt engines with upgraded components offer simplest reliable solution
Cross-fuel-type swaps require ECU, wiring and emissions system compatibility verification
Supercharger additions to naturally aspirated AJ133 units need specialist calibration work
Chassis mounting points and transmission compatibility must be verified before any upgrade
items.cta: Enquire about Jaguar XJ engine upgrade options with UK specialists today

items.question: What's included in a Jaguar XJ reconditioned engine with warranty?
items.answer: A Jaguar XJ reconditioned engine with warranty typically includes full disassembly, ultrasonic cleaning, replacement of all seals/gaskets, upgraded timing components addressing known failures, and bench testing before dispatch. Minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty covers parts and labour, with supply-and-fit options available through EngineMarket's vetted UK specialist network.
keyPoints: Full strip-down and ultrasonic cleaning removes carbon buildup and contamination
All seals, gaskets, belts and water pumps replaced as standard procedure
Upgraded timing chain guides and tensioners fitted addressing known Jaguar XJ weaknesses
Bench testing verifies compression and oil pressure before UK network dispatch
items.cta: Compare reconditioned Jaguar XJ engines with 12-month warranty from UK specialists

items.question: How do I know which engine code fits my Jaguar XJ?
items.answer: Identify your Jaguar XJ engine code by checking the VIN plate on the firewall, service book, or using EngineMarket's free registration lookup tool. Common codes include AJ133 (5.0L V8 petrol), AJD-V6/306DT (3.0L diesel), and AJ200D (2.0L Ingenium diesel). Matching the exact code ensures compatibility and avoids costly fitment errors during replacement.
keyPoints: VIN plate on driver-side firewall displays engine code stamp clearly
Service book and MOT certificates often record engine code for verification
AJ133, AJD-V6 and AJ200D are the most common Jaguar XJ codes
Registration lookup instantly confirms engine code, variant and compatible replacements
items.cta: Use our free registration checker to identify your Jaguar XJ engine code instantly
